sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Nottingham Forest have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins and 3 losses, including a strong 3-0 victory over Tottenham but a recent 1-2 defeat to Manchester City. Everton, on the other hand, are struggling, winless in their last 3 matches with 2 losses and a draw, failing to score in those games. This suggests Forest might have a slight edge in momentum coming into this match at the City Ground.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Everton have dominated, winning both matches with a combined score of 4-0, including a 3-0 victory on December 6, 2025. Historically, Everton have won 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads, which could give them psychological confidence despite their recent poor form.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Nottingham Forest, sitting 17th with 18 points, have struggled offensively (18 goals, 18th in the league) and defensively (28 goals conceded). Everton, positioned 12th with 25 points, have also scored 18 goals but have a slightly better defensive record (20 conceded). Forest's ability to create penalty area entries (5th in the league) could be a key factor against Everton's defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Forest will rely on players like Callum Hudson-Odoi and Morgan Gibbs-White for creativity, but they miss key midfielder Ibrahim Sangaré. Everton are without several influential players, including Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall and Iliman Ndiaye, which could hamper their attack. James Tarkowski remains a defensive stalwart for the Toffees with a high Sofascore rating in recent matches against Forest.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Forest tend to score more in the second half (especially 46-60 minutes), which could see them push for late goals if the game is tight. Everton have a balanced goal distribution but have been first to score in 5 of their last 6 head-to-heads against Forest. Expect a physical game with a high likelihood of cards, as 7 of the last 9 head-to-heads had over 4.5 cards.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Despite Everton's recent head-to-head dominance, their current form and missing players tilt the balance slightly towards Nottingham Forest at home. Sofascore predicts a 40% chance of a home win, 25% draw, and 35% away win, which aligns with Forest's potential to capitalize on Everton's struggles. A narrow victory for Forest seems plausible, though a draw remains a strong possibility given both teams' inconsistencies.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Everton holds a narrow 0-1 lead over Nottingham Forest at the City Ground. James Garner’s well-taken goal in the 19th minute has given the visitors the edge. Despite dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third and penalty area, Forest have struggled to convert their chances, missing a big opportunity. Everton, on the other hand, have been clinical with their limited opportunities, making the most of their attacking moment to secure the lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Everton showing early intent, maintaining a strong attacking presence in the opening minutes. However, momentum shifted to Nottingham Forest around the 11th minute, as they began to assert control with sustained attacking spells for much of the half. Forest enjoyed several prolonged periods of pressure, particularly in the middle and latter stages of the half. Everton had brief moments of resurgence, especially around the time of their goal, but largely adopted a more defensive posture as Forest pushed forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance insights
halftimeSections.text: Nottingham Forest have controlled the ball for the majority of the first half, with significant dominance in possession and frequent entries into dangerous areas. Despite creating a big chance, their finishing has let them down, with a key opportunity squandered. Their duel success rate is balanced, but they’ve struggled in aerial battles. Everton, conversely, have been more effective with less of the ball, capitalizing on a big chance to take the lead. Their aerial dominance has been a notable strength, and defensively, they’ve held firm against Forest’s pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Nottingham Forest, Callum Hudson-Odoi has been a constant threat, showing intent in attack despite missing a big chance. Neco Williams has also stood out with his creativity, contributing significantly to Forest’s attacking plays. On Everton’s side, James Garner has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also making key contributions defensively. Dwight McNeil’s assist and overall involvement in build-up play have been vital for the Toffees.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Nottingham Forest are likely to come out with renewed intensity in the second half, given their dominance in possession and territory in the first 45 minutes. They’ll need to sharpen their finishing to overturn the deficit. Everton, meanwhile, may look to exploit counter-attacking opportunities while maintaining their solid defensive structure. Forest’s persistence could see them level the score, but Everton’s efficiency in front of goal makes them dangerous on the break. A tight contest is expected, with both sides capable of finding the net.
